prompt: A vertical commercial poster for a ramen specialty shop called "Gokumen". Left side: A profile of a middle-aged Japanese male chef with a sturdy build, shaved head, and black cooking attire. He is in the middle of the 'Yugiri' action, lifting noodles with a strainer. Dramatic steam billows around him. Dark kitchen setting with cinematic lighting. Texture like a professional documentary photograph. Right side: A large vertical catchphrase in white text: "In the cauldron of hell, I boil again today." Below that in small text: Gokumen Owner Enji Gokuda. Background: Almost black with a misty atmosphere. Bottom 30% area: An off-white cream-colored section. Small vertical interview text in a newspaper or magazine-style column layout: "All customers who come to this shop come with resolve. There are no excuses for the spiciness. I have packed my hell into every bowl of soup. If you want to run away, go ahead. But once you eat it—there's no going back." Small elegant Mincho font in multiple columns. Overall: A premium dark editorial poster for a ramen shop.
